name: Deluxe Chocolate Cake
recipeIngredient: 1 cup light brown sugar, packed (200 grams)
recipeInstructions:
For the cake:
Adjust rack in lower third of oven. Preheat oven to 350°F.
Grease and flour two 8-inch round cake pans, and insert parchment paper or waxed paper to line the bottoms.
Sift the flour, baking soda, and salt onto a sheet of waxed paper; set aside.
Place cocoa in a 1-quart mixing bowl. Add 1/2 cup lukewarm water, and whisk to combine; set aside to cool.
Pour buttermilk, 1/2 cup water, and vanilla into a liquid measuring cup.
Crack eggs into a small bowl, and whisk together to combine the yolks and whites.
Place butter in the bowl of a heavy-duty mixer, preferably fitted with a flat beater. Cream butter on medium speed until smooth and lighter in color, about 30 to 45 seconds.
Reduce speed to low, add sugars in a steady stream. When all sugar is added, stop machine, and scrape mixture clinging to side of bowl into center. The mixture will appear sandy.
Increase speed to medium, and cream until mixture is light in color, fluffy in texture, and appears as one mass, about 5 to 7 minutes.
With mixer on medium speed, pour eggs slowly at first. Continue to cream, scraping sides of bowl at least once, until mixture appears fluffy and velvety.
Stop machine, spoon in cooled cocoa mixture, resume mixing at medium speed, and mix just until incorporated.
Using rubber spatula, stir in 1/4 cup flour mixture. Then 1/3 buttermilk mixture; repeat with remaining additions, alternating dry and liquid, scraping sides of bowl and continuing mixing until smooth, never adding liquid if any flour is visible.
Pour batter into pans and spread level. Bake for 25 minutes, or until surface springs back slightly when touched lightly in center and sides contract from pan.
Place cake on rack to cool for 5 to 10 minutes. With mitts, gently tilt and rotate pans, tapping them on counter to check if cake releases from sides.
Yield: Two 8-inch layers
Instructions for the filling:
Put chocolate in a medium bowl. In small saucepan, heat heavy cream just to boil. Remove from heat. Pour over chocolate and whisk until chocolate melts and mixture is smooth and shiny.
Assembling the dessert:
Split each layer of cake horizontally and place one layer, cut side up, on cardboard round. Spread with 1/3 ganache filling.
Center second layer on top of first, spread with remaining ganache filling. Place another layer, cut side up, on top, and spread remaining filling. Turn last layer upside down and center it over filling.
Frosting and Chocolate Glaze:
Frost cake with whipped cream. Set cake on wire rack, then sheet pan that fits dimensions of freezer. Freeze for 40 minutes only (enough to chill whipped cream frosting firm to touch, not soft).
While cake is freezing, prepare chocolate glaze by putting chocolate in medium bowl. In small saucepan, heat heavy cream just to boil. Remove from heat. Pour over chocolate and whisk until smooth and shiny.
Set aside glaze to cool to body temperature. Place cake on wire rack over sheet pan with sides (like jelly roll pan) on top of turntable or lazy Susan. Pour almost all glaze over center of cake. Using long, flexible metal icing spatula, spread glaze over top so it runs down sides.
Rotate turntable as you spread glaze and use spatula to scoop up excess glaze to cover any bare spots on sides. Place cake on serving plate and refrigerate until 30 to 60 minutes before serving.
Using serrated knife dipped in hot water after each slice, cut dessert into wedges.
